meaning: n.鑰佸紡鐨勫ぇ鍙ｅ緞鐭灙,锠㈡潗,杞荤巼鐨勪汉
examples: You'll take me blunderbuss we ye pry it from my cold, dead hands!
The trouble is that Bernanke's stimulus spending was a blunderbuss.
There's two versions, a standard pistol and a blunderbuss that's a more effective short-ranged weapon.
Mr. Harper's blunderbuss condemnation of most large firms and most law schools is off-target.
One thing he will discover: Setting interest rates is a clumsy, blunderbuss, hit-or-miss way to conduct monetary policy.
